scenarios.description: children() on element with no child nodes returns empty Elements (childNodeSize==0 branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() returns single child element when exactly one Element child present (childNodeSize>0, loop-1)
scenarios.expectedResult:
returned Elements contains exactly the

element (size 1 and element.tagName()=="p")

scenarios.description: children() filters out non-Element nodes and returns only Element children (mix of Element and TextNode)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("a"); parent.appendText("x"); parent.appendElement("span");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains only two Elements in order [<a>,<span>] (size 2)
pseudocode: assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0).tagName().equals("a"); assert result.get(1).tagName().equals("span");

scenarios.description: children() caches childElementsList; second call uses cache without rebuilding (shadowChildrenRef non-null branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("p"); // build cache on first call
scenarios.expectedResult: first and second children() calls return Elements backed by the same List instance

scenarios.description: children() invalidates cache after node list change and rebuilds list including new child
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("p"); // sets cache; then parent.appendElement("span") invalidates cache
scenarios.expectedResult:
children() after mutation returns list of two Elements [

,]
